title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Automation and Data Cli… · Runtime Lens: Request Field Injection
summary: A server-side preview fetcher accepts source URLs and must defeat SSRF, DNS rebinding, redirect bypass, hostile response data, and unsafe logging. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py request field injection increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
